mainEntity.name: How much does an AJ-V8 4.2/4.4 Supercharged engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ-V8 supercharged typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only, with used units from £2,500–£4,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000 depending on labour rates and supercharger plumbing complexity.

mainEntity.name: Is the AJ-V8 4.2/4.4 Supercharged a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ-V8 has a mixed reputation (3.5★). It offers glorious V8 power and a legendary soundtrack, but pre‑2012 examples are known for cooling system issues and timing chain guide wear. Post‑2012 revisions addressed many of these concerns.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common AJ-V8 supercharged failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system leaks are the most common — including water pump failure and coolant crossover pipe corrosion. Timing chain guide wear (pre‑2012) and supercharger coupler wear are also well‑documented. Oil leaks from valve covers are common on higher-mileage examples.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the AJ-V8 supercharged?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 4.2 SC powered the Range Rover L322 (2005–2009) and Range Rover Sport L320 (2006–2009). The 4.4 SC powered the L322 (2010–2012) and early L405 (2012–2013).

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover AJ-V8 the same as Jaguar's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the AJ-V8 supercharged engine is shared with Jaguar fitments in the XKR, XFR, XJ, and XK. The engine is mechanically identical, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.

items.question: How much does an AJ‑V8 4.2/4.4 Supercharged engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned AJ‑V8 supercharged typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only, with used units from £2,500–£4,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000 depending on labour rates and supercharger plumbing complexity.

items.question: Is the AJ‑V8 4.2/4.4 Supercharged a reliable engine?
items.answer: The AJ‑V8 has a mixed reputation (3.5★). It offers glorious V8 power and a legendary soundtrack, but pre‑2012 examples are known for cooling system issues (water pump, crossover pipes) and timing chain guide wear. Post‑2012 revisions addressed many of these concerns.

items.question: What is the most common AJ‑V8 supercharged failure?
items.answer: Cooling system leaks are the most common — including water pump failure, thermostat housing cracks, and coolant crossover pipe corrosion. Timing chain guide wear (pre‑2012) and supercharger coupler wear (causing rattles) are also well‑documented. Oil leaks from the valve covers are common on higher‑mileage examples.

items.question: Which Land Rover models use the AJ‑V8 supercharged?
items.answer: The 4.2 SC powered the Range Rover L322 (2005–2009) and Range Rover Sport L320 (2006–2009). The 4.4 SC powered the L322 (2010–2012) and early L405 (2012–2013).

items.question: Is the Land Rover AJ‑V8 the same as Jaguar's version?
items.answer: Yes — the AJ‑V8 supercharged engine is shared with Jaguar fitments in the XKR, XFR, XJ, and XK. The engine is mechanically identical, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.
